ops.title: Drain refuter findings (sol xhigh, 2026-07-18): 5 broken properties + disposition
ops.body:
Adversarial refuter pass (gpt-5.6-sol xhigh, read-only) on drain-transition ordering at f4cd5e18 (includes 3d55ae2e). Verdict: the 1→8 normative order is syntactically present and several properties hold (admission closure, settle barrier, all-or-old journal replacement on clean crash, sequential foreign-journal rejection, strict ordinary CAS, D4, uncancelable D5 Apply, D5 row-not-advanced on clean Apply error) — but the contract fails under adversarial scheduling and storage crashes.

**Findings + orchestrator disposition:**

- **F1 CRITICAL, accepted — no durable commit boundary.** No fsync anywhere in daemon//drain/: StateFile.write (daemon/state.go:79-106) renames unsynced; WriteOwner (drain/journal.go:287) is plain os.WriteFile; AdoptDead applies to canonical then RemoveAll's the generation with no sync between (drain/scan.go:138). A storage crash can persist the canonical truncate while losing the generation journal → the only copy of a row vanishes. FIX (bounded, sol lane): fsync file+dir in StateFile.write and WriteOwner; AdoptDead syncs canonical before RemoveAll. Mitigation note: for fusekit mounts the resource dies with the host too, and force always requires kernel-truth probing — but drain is generic and consumers may own durable resources.
- **F2 CRITICAL, accepted — no already-draining refusal.** Transition (drain/transition.go:148,193) has no transition-wide serialization: two Transitions to DIFFERENT generation names both snapshot the same canonical rows and both take readable ownership → double-owned rows; ResolveOwner can't arbitrate two generations. FIX (bounded): step 1's draining flag becomes a CAS — a second Transition refuses while one is in flight/unrecovered.
- **F3 HIGH, accepted — D2 TOCTOU.** Foreign-journal empty-check and snapshot Apply take separate flocks (transition.go:193-225, journal.go:53,81); a disjoint foreign row applied between them survives the applied-count check and WriteOwner overwrites the foreign owner. FIX (bounded): one held flock across check+apply+owner-write.
- **F4 HIGH, accepted as design gap — no global ownership arbitration.** An older generation's live runner can yield/apply a row after a newer Transition snapshotted the same key from canonical (run.go:178-201; CAS is per-journal). Partially mitigated by fence + kernel-truth law, but the cross-journal resolver absence is real. DESIGN DECISION (fable, remaining-work doc): options are registration-time refusal of keys owned by a live generation, or a global resolver/CAS.
- **F5 HIGH, accepted — fence release can fail and leak forever.** run.go:361-365 logs and discards fence.Release() errors; interface permits a still-held fence → row permanently unretryable. DESIGN DECISION, small: either contract Release as infallible/idempotent, or crash the process on release failure (process exit releases OS-level fences — matches crash-on-unexpected). Test gap bundled: fakeFence can't inject release failure and never flips held.
- **F6 MEDIUM, accepted in two parts.** (a) MUST-FIX: AdoptDead ignores the applied count before RemoveAll — at Seq=MaxUint64 the bump wraps to 0, CAS suppresses, and the generation is deleted anyway → row lost. Check applied count before destroying the last copy. (b) cheap hardening: saturate/refuse at max instead of wrapping (Bump, journal.go:111).
- **F7 MEDIUM, accepted as design choice — pre-owner crash poisons the generation name.** Ownerless-complete generation is Undetermined forever; same-name retry refuses. No data loss (canonical still authoritative). Options: recovery removes provably-never-owned generations, or nonce'd generation names. Remaining-work doc.
- **F8 LOW, accepted — flock release unobservable.** proc/flock.go:23 Release discards unlock/close errors; Transition spawns the successor regardless. FIX (bounded): Release returns error; Transition fails loudly.

**Test-suite gaps to close with the fixes:** crash-point tests inject orderly returns at labeled hooks, never real durability/partial-write crashes; three semantic step-swaps pass the suite (truncate moved pre-owner-write inside snapshot; CloseIntake/Intake.Close gap; bind-before-truncate with delayed marker); no test covers two generation names, an older live runner vs newer transition, max-seq overflow, or fence-release failure.

Fix routing: F1/F2/F3/F6a/F8 + suite strengthening = one bounded sol hardening lane (queued behind the CI-hang fix lane to avoid working-copy collision). F5 = small design pick then same lane. F4/F7 = fable design decisions for the remaining-work plan.
